input.source_name: PubMed Abstract - Low-dose atropine for myopia control
input.text: Low-dose atropine for myopia progression control in children: a systematic review and meta-analysis. Purpose: To evaluate the efficacy and safety of different concentrations of atropine for myopia control. Methods: We searched PubMed, Embase, and Cochrane Library for randomized controlled trials (RCTs) comparing atropine with placebo for myopia control in children. Results: Twelve RCTs with 2,856 participants were included. Atropine 0.01% significantly reduced myopia progression (mean difference: -0.53 D/year, 95% CI: -0.62 to -0.44) compared with placebo. Atropine 0.05% showed greater efficacy (-0.71 D/year) but with higher incidence of photophobia (15.3% vs 3.2% for 0.01%). No serious adverse events were reported. Conclusion: Low-dose atropine 0.01% provides an optimal balance between efficacy and safety for pediatric myopia control. Higher concentrations offer greater myopia control but with more side effects.
